samples.xml_description: Implement a module of a carry-lookahead 32-bit adder that uses the Carry-Lookahead Adder (CLA) architecture. 32-bit input operand A. 32-bit input operand B. 32-bit output representing the sum of A and B. Carry-out output. 32 The adder_32bit consists of 2 16-bit carry-lookahead adders. The first adder computes the sum of the lower 16 bits of A and B and its carry-out is connected to the carry-in of the second adder. The first adder is implemented in the generated code. The second adder computes the sum of the upper 16 bits of A and B and its carry-out is connected to the carry-in of the first adder. The second adder is implemented using the CLA architecture. The implementation of the CLA architecture is as follows: wire [N-1:1] P, G; wire [N:1] C; assign P = A ^ B; assign G = A & B; assign C[1] = G[1] | (P[1] & Cin); assign S[1] = P[1] ^ Cin; genvar i; generate for (i = 2; i <= N; i = i + 1) begin: adder assign C[i] = G[i] | (P[i] & C[i-1]); assign S[i] = P[i] ^ C[i-1]; end endgenerate
